blocks.tagline: Why can two visually identical sets of Sitka spruce sound markedly different? PRT's Dr. David Olson and Eric Warner on sonic grading — density, stiffness, damping — and the listening studies behind it.

blocks.html:
Aired on Looth Group Live, Tuesday, April 28th, 2026.

Dr. David Olson and Eric Warner of Pacific Rim Tonewoods join Looth Group Live to examine a familiar but not fully understood phenomenon: why two visually similar sets of Sitka spruce can exhibit markedly different acoustic responses. What material properties are driving those differences?

Pacific Rim Tonewoods, based in Washington, supplies tonewood to a wide range of contemporary builders. Dr. Olson brings a distinctive interdisciplinary background to the discussion: Director of Research at PRT, otologic surgeon, former forest ecologist, and co-author of acoustics research conducted in TU Dresden’s anechoic chamber.

Together, David and Eric explore sonic grading — what it quantifies, how measurements are taken, and how well those metrics correlate with the perceptual evaluations luthiers have relied on at the bench.

blocks.caption: Pacific Rim Tonewoods with Dr. Olson.

blocks.html:
00:00:00 — Introduction: Pacific Rim Tonewoods & Sonic Grading Overview

00:02:47 — Background: Forest Ecology, Acoustics Research & PRT Collaboration

00:05:12 — Milling Practices & Why Quartering Matters

00:07:44 — Inherent Variability in Wood: Species Tendencies vs Realities

00:11:22 — What Gets Measured: Density, Stiffness & Damping Explained

00:15:01 — Why Damping Matters: Moisture, Sustain & the Bill Collings Observation

00:17:11 — How the Ear Hears: FFT, the Cochlea & Why Machines Can't Judge Guitars

00:19:35 — The Taylor Guitar Study: Building Test Guitars with Known Properties

00:21:16 — Player Tests at Taylor & Listening Tests in Dresden

00:24:08 — Results: Low Density & Low Stiffness Consistently Preferred

00:28:38 — Q&A: Listener Expertise, Playing Style & Guitar Optimization

00:34:33 — The Q Factor Study: High Q vs Low Q Tops

00:39:00 — Acoustic Rating Protocol & How Builders Can Use This Data

00:42:38 — Q&A: Relationship Between Q and Density, Measuring Challenges

00:51:44 — Lutz Spruce Genetics, Hybrid Species & Sound Radiation Coefficient

00:56:09 — Q&A: Surface Prep Effects on Q, Book-Matched Sets & Top Deflection

01:01:08 — Closing Thanks
